org.arakhne.neteditor.android.activity

Class FigureView<G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • android.view.View
      • org.arakhne.afc.ui.android.zoom.ZoomableView
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.android.activity.FigureView<G>
  • Type Parameters:
    G - is the type of the graph supported by this editor.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    android.graphics.drawable.Drawable.Callback, android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEventSource, android.view.KeyEvent.Callback, Serializable, Iterable<Figure>, ZoomableContext, ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G>

    public class FigureView<G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
extends ZoomableView
implements ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G>
    This view permits to draw NetEditor figures on an Droid view.
    Version:
    NetEditor Android Panel $Revision$ 2013-10-23 21:03:10
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-androidpanel

      Add listener on figure events.

      Compute and reply the bounds of the entire document.

      Invoked by the constructor to create a wrapper to the data of the viewed document.
      protected void fireFigureAdded(Figure added)
      Notifies the listeners about the addition of a figure.
      protected void fireFigureChanged(Figure changed)
      Notifies the listeners about the change of a figure.
      protected void fireFigureRemoved(Figure removed)
      Notifies the listeners about the removal of a figure.
      FigureActionModeManager getActionModeManager()
      Replies the object that permits to change the configuration of the action mode manager.
      Color getBackgroundColor()
      Replies the background color of the panel.
      CollisionAvoider getCollisionAvoider()
      Replies the object that permits to avoid collision between a figure to insert inside and the already present figures.
      Figure getFigureAt(float x, float y)
      Replies the figure at the given coordinate.
      Figure getFigureAt(int index)
      Replies the element at the specified index.
      int getFigureCount()
      Replies the number of components in this container.
      FigureFactory<G> getFigureFactory()
      Replies the figure factory used by this panel.
      Figure getFigureIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
      Replies the first figure that are inside the specified bounds.
      Figure getFigureOn(Shape2f bounds)
      Replies the first figure that are intersecting the specified bounds.
      Collection<? extends Figure> getFigures()
      Replies the figures in the container.
      Set<Figure> getFiguresIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
      Replies the figures that are inside the specified bounds.
      Set<Figure> getFiguresOn(Shape2f bounds)
      Replies the figures that are intersecting the specified bounds.
      Figure getFigureWithBoundsAt(float x, float y)
      Replies the figure that has its bounds with the specified point inside.
      G getGraph()
      Replies the graph displayed by this view.
      float getHitPrecision()
      Replies the precisions of the hits on the view.
      protected float getPreferredFocusX()
      Replies the preferred position of the focus point.
      protected float getPreferredFocusY()
      Replies the preferred position of the focus point.
      Color getSelectionBackgroundColor()
      Replies the background color associated to selection.
      Color getSelectionForegroundColor()
      Replies the foreground color associated to selection.
      SelectionManager getSelectionManager()
      Replies the selection manager.
      Object getTreeLock()
      Gets this container's locking object (the object that owns the thread synchronization monitor) for rendering tree and layout operations.
      UndoManager getUndoManager()
      Replies the undo/redo manager.
      UUID getUUID()
      Replies the UUId of the view.
      Rectangle2f getViewBounds()
      Replies the bounds of the view of the components.
      void importGraph(G graph, Map<UUID,List<ViewComponent>> figures)
      Import the graph and the specified figures into this editor.
      int indexOfFigure(Figure figure)
      Replies the index of the figure.
      boolean isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects()
      Replies if the mode container want to remove the model objects when a figure is removed from the view.
      boolean isEditable()
      Replies if the data inside the view is editable.
      boolean isFigureAutomaticallyAdded()
      Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph.
      boolean isSelectionEnabled()
      Replies if the selection manager is enabled.
      boolean isShadowDrawn()
      Replies if the shadows are drawn.
      Iterator<Figure> iterator()
      int moveFigureAt(Figure figure, int index)
      Move the figure at the specified position in the layers used by this panel.

      Move the figure at the background in the layers used by this panel.
      void moveFigureDown(Figure figure)
      Move the figure down in the layers used by this panel.
      void moveFigureFront(Figure figure)
      Move the figure at the front in the layers used by this panel.
      void moveFigureUp(Figure figure)
      Move the figure up in the layers used by this panel.

      Invoked when a short-click is detected.

      Invoked when the a touch-up event is detected.

    • Method Detail

      • createDocumentWrapper

        protected DocumentWrapper createDocumentWrapper()
        Description copied from class: ZoomableView
        Invoked by the constructor to create a wrapper to the data of the viewed document. If there is no wrapper, several functions of ZoomableView will not work.
        Specified by:
        createDocumentWrapper in class ZoomableView
        Returns:
        the wrapper or null.

      • isSelectionEnabled

        public boolean isSelectionEnabled()
        Replies if the selection manager is enabled.
        Returns:
        true if the selection manager is enabled.

      • setSelectionEnabled

        public void setSelectionEnabled(boolean enable)
        Set if the selection manager is enabled.
        Parameters:
        enable - is true if the selection manager is enabled.

      • isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects

        public boolean isAlwaysRemovingModelObjects()
        Replies if the mode container want to remove the model objects when a figure is removed from the view.
        Returns:
        true if both the figure and the model objects should be removed at the same time, false if only the figure should be removed (not the model object).

      • getSelectionManager

        public SelectionManager getSelectionManager()
        Replies the selection manager.
        Returns:
        the selection manager.

        public UndoManager getUndoManager()
        Replies the undo/redo manager.
        Returns:
        the undo/redo manager.

        public void setSelectionManager(SelectionManager manager)
        Set the selection manager.
        Parameters:
        manager - is the new selection manager.

        Replies the UUId of the view.

        the uuid of the view.

        protected float getPreferredFocusX()
        Replies the preferred position of the focus point.
        Specified by:
        getPreferredFocusX in class ZoomableView
        Returns:
        the preferred position of the focus point.

        Replies the preferred position of the focus point.

        getPreferredFocusY in class ZoomableView

      • getCollisionAvoider

        public CollisionAvoider getCollisionAvoider()
        Replies the object that permits to avoid collision between a figure to insert inside and the already present figures.
        Returns:
        the collision avoider.

      • isFigureAutomaticallyAdded

        public boolean isFigureAutomaticallyAdded()
        Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph. The figures are created with the figure factory replied by getFigureFactory().
        Returns:
        true if the figures are automatically added; otherwise false.

      • setFigureAutomaticallyAdded

        public void setFigureAutomaticallyAdded(boolean auto)
        Replies if the figure are automatically added into the view when a model object was added in the graph. The figures are created with the figure factory replied by getFigureFactory().
        Parameters:
        auto - is true if the figures are automatically added; otherwise false.

      • getFigureFactory

        public FigureFactory<G> getFigureFactory()
        Replies the figure factory used by this panel.
        Specified by:
        getFigureFactory in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        the figure factory.

      • setFigureFactory

        public void setFigureFactory(FigureFactory<G> factory)
        Set the figure factory used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        factory - is the new figure factory.

      • getGraph

        public G getGraph()
        Replies the graph displayed by this view.
        Returns:
        the graph.

      • setGraph

        public void setGraph(G g)
        Set the graph associated to this panel.
        Parameters:
        g - is the graph.

      • getFigures

        public Collection<? extends Figure> getFigures()
        Replies the figures in the container.
        Returns:
        the figures.

      • getFigureWithBoundsAt

        public Figure getFigureWithBoundsAt(float x,
                           float y)
        Replies the figure that has its bounds with the specified point inside. If you want to test the point against the shape of the figures, please use getFigureAt(float, float). This function uses the precision of the clicks, which is replied by getHitPrecision().
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null if none.

      • getFiguresOn

        public Set<Figure> getFiguresOn(Shape2f bounds)
        Replies the figures that are intersecting the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figures, never null.

      • getFigureOn

        public Figure getFigureOn(Shape2f bounds)
        Replies the first figure that are intersecting the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figure, or null.

      • getFiguresIn

        public Set<Figure> getFiguresIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Replies the figures that are inside the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figures, never null.

      • getFigureIn

        public Figure getFigureIn(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Replies the first figure that are inside the specified bounds.
        Parameters:
        bounds -
        Returns:
        the hit figures, never null.

      • moveFigureUp

        public void moveFigureUp(Figure figure)
        Move the figure up in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move up.

      • moveFigureDown

        public void moveFigureDown(Figure figure)
        Move the figure down in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move down.

      • moveFigureFront

        public void moveFigureFront(Figure figure)
        Move the figure at the front in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the front.

      • moveFigureBackground

        public void moveFigureBackground(Figure figure)
        Move the figure at the background in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the background.

      • moveFigureAt

        public int moveFigureAt(Figure figure,
               int index)
        Move the figure at the specified position in the layers used by this panel.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to move to the front.
        index - is the new position of the figure.
        Returns:
        the previous position of the figure; or -1 if the figure was not found.
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException

      • indexOfFigure

        public int indexOfFigure(Figure figure)
        Replies the index of the figure. This index is also the position of the from the background.
        Parameters:
        figure - is the figure to search for.
        Returns:
        the position of the figure; -1 if is was not found.

      • getViewBounds

        public final Rectangle2f getViewBounds()
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Replies the bounds of the view of the components.
        Specified by:
        getViewBounds in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        the bounds of the view of the components.

      • calcDocumentBounds

        protected Rectangle2f calcDocumentBounds()
        Compute and reply the bounds of the entire document.
        Returns:
        the bounds of the entire document.

      • iterator

        public Iterator<Figure> iterator()
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface Iterable<Figure>

      • getHitPrecision

        public float getHitPrecision()
        Replies the precisions of the hits on the view.
        Returns:
        the precisions of the hits.

      • setHitPrecision

        public void setHitPrecision(float precision)
        Replies the precision of the hits on the view. Byt default, the value of CLICK_PRECISION is used.
        Parameters:
        precision - is the precision of the hits, in pixels.

      • isEditable

        public boolean isEditable()
        Replies if the data inside the view is editable.
        Returns:
        true if the view enables edition; otherwise false.

      • setEditable

        public void setEditable(boolean editable)
        Set if this viewer is interactively editable.
        Parameters:
        editable - is true if this viewer is editable; otherwise false.

      • isShadowDrawn

        public boolean isShadowDrawn()
        Replies if the shadows are drawn.
        Specified by:
        isShadowDrawn in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        true if the shadows are drawn; otherwise false.

      • setShadowDrawn

        public void setShadowDrawn(boolean draw)
        Set if the shadows are drawn.
        Specified by:
        setShadowDrawn in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        draw - is true if the shadows are drawn; otherwise false.

      • repaint

        public void repaint(Figure figure)
        Refresh the drawing area covered by the given figure.

        This function does nothing if ZoomableView.isIgnoreRepaint() replies true;

        Parameters:
        figure -

      • onDrawView

        protected void onDrawView(android.graphics.Canvas canvas,
              float scaleFactor,
              CenteringTransform centeringTransform)
        Invoked to paint the view after it is translated and scaled.
        Specified by:
        onDrawView in class ZoomableView
        Parameters:
        canvas - is the canvas in which the view must be painted.
        scaleFactor - is the scaling factor to use for drawing.
        centeringTransform - is the transform to use to put the draws at the center of the view.

      • paintViewComponents

        public void paintViewComponents(ViewGraphics2D g)
        Method to paint the figures.
        Specified by:
        paintViewComponents in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        g - the graphic context.

        onPointerPressed in class ZoomableView

      • getTreeLock

        public Object getTreeLock()
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Gets this container's locking object (the object that owns the thread synchronization monitor) for rendering tree and layout operations.
        Specified by:
        getTreeLock in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        this component's locking object

      • getFigureCount

        public int getFigureCount()
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Replies the number of components in this container.
        Specified by:
        getFigureCount in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        the number of components in this container.

      • getFigureAt

        public Figure getFigureAt(float x,
                 float y)
        Replies the figure at the given coordinate. This function is testing the point against the shapes of the figures. If you want to test the bounds of the figure, use getFigureWithBoundsAt(float, float). This function uses the precision of the clicks, which is replied by getHitPrecision().
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -
        Returns:
        the figure at the given coordinate; or null if none.

      • getFigureAt

        public Figure getFigureAt(int index)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Replies the element at the specified index.
        Specified by:
        getFigureAt in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        index - is the index of the element.
        Returns:
        the element at the specified index.

      • addFigure

        public int addFigure(Figure component)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Add the element at the specified index.
        Specified by:
        addFigure in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to add.
        Returns:
        the position where the figure is inserted; or -1 if the figure was not added.

      • removeFigure

        public int removeFigure(Figure component)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Remove the specified element.
        Specified by:
        removeFigure in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to remove.
        Returns:
        the position of the removed figure.

      • removeFigure

        public int removeFigure(Figure component,
               boolean unlinkToModelObject)
        Remove the specified component.
        Parameters:
        component - is the component to remove.
        unlinkToModelObject - indicates if the removed component should be unlink to its model object.
        Returns:
        position of the removed figure; or -1 if not found.
        See Also:
        removeFigure(Figure)

      • removeFigureAt

        public Figure removeFigureAt(int index)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Remove the element at the specified index.
        Specified by:
        removeFigureAt in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        index - is the position of the element to remove.
        Returns:
        the removed figure.

        public void removeAllFigures()
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponentContainer
        Clear the components.
        Specified by:
        removeAllFigures in interface ViewComponentContainer<Figure,G extends Graph<?,?,?,?>>

      • importGraph

        public void importGraph(G graph,
               Map<UUID,List<ViewComponent>> figures)
        Import the graph and the specified figures into this editor.
        Parameters:
        graph - is the graph to integrate.
        figures - are the figures (classified per view id) to import, from front to background.
